name: Quark mass spectrum: Froggatt-Nielsen compression
requirement: Any candidate flavor mechanism must reproduce the six quark masses (and ideally three charged-lepton masses) by compressing them into a small set of inputs. The Froggatt-Nielsen ansatz y_ij ‾ epsilon^{q_i + q_j} with epsilon ‾ 0.22 and integer left-handed/right-handed charges fits the observed Yukawa hierarchy at the order-of-magnitude level with a single continuous parameter epsilon and ‾6 integer charges.
what_it_excludes: A flavor mechanism that produces only flat or random Yukawa structure cannot reproduce the geometric hierarchy. A mechanism that introduces ‾9 independent free parameters (one per Yukawa) achieves no compression; the benchmark scores it as a non-explanation.

regime.description: Top-quark mass scale; Yukawas are run-dependent but the hierarchical structure is preserved across renormalization-group flow.

parameterization.name: Froggatt-Nielsen abelian flavor U(1)
parameterization.reference: Froggatt and Nielsen, Nucl. Phys. B 147, 277 (1979)
parameterization.summary: Each fermion carries an integer charge q_i under U(1)_FN. After the flavon picks up VEV <phi>/Lambda = epsilon, Yukawa couplings y_ij ‾ epsilon^{|q_i + q_j|} arise from the leading allowed operator. Hierarchy is geometric in the integer charges.

procedural.predicted_observable: y_ii(M) ‾ epsilon^{2 q_i} for diagonal Yukawas; off-diagonals follow from charge sums.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: epsilon ‾ 0.22 (Cabibbo angle) with integer charges {q_Q1, q_Q2, q_Q3, q_u1, q_u2, q_u3, q_d1, q_d2, q_d3} chosen to reproduce y_t ‾ 1, y_c ‾ epsilon^4, y_u ‾ epsilon^6-7, y_b ‾ epsilon^3, y_s ‾ epsilon^5, y_d ‾ epsilon^7. Charge assignments are not unique but are highly constrained.
framework_inputs: Predicted scale of expansion parameter epsilon
Predicted set of integer charges per fermion species
RG-running boundary conditions if Yukawas are predicted at a scale other than M_Z
procedural.compression_ansatz: y_ij(M_high) = c_ij * epsilon^{|q_Q_i + q_R_j|} with c_ij = O(1) coefficients and integer q_Q_i, q_R_j; running to M_Z reproduces the measured PDG masses to within factor-of-few.
free_parameters: {q_Q_i, q_u_i, q_d_i} (nine integers; constrained but not unique)
O(1) coefficients c_ij (treated as anarchic; nine moduli)
procedural.notes: Compression ratio: six measured masses + nine off-diagonal Yukawas reduced to one continuous parameter + ‾9 integers + O(1) anarchic coefficients. Treat the integers as discrete data and the c_ij as not contributing to the explanation; the six logarithmic spreads then collapse to one (epsilon).

primary_references.citation: Froggatt and Nielsen, 'Hierarchy of quark masses, Cabibbo angles and CP violation', Nucl. Phys. B 147, 277 (1979)
Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics' (2024)
Leurer, Nir, Seiberg, 'Mass matrix models', Nucl. Phys. B 398, 319 (1993)
